discovery.run_summary: Broad risk-off session with VIX up sharply and most sectors red after Warsh's first Fed meeting removed the cutting bias and several officials signaled a potential 2026 hike. Notable divergence: SMH and XBI are green against the tape (AVGO +4.3% leading semis, biotech rallying on UniQure FDA news). Mid-cap movers split between biotech beneficiaries and pockets of weakness in consumer cyclicals and software.

notable_index_moves: VIX +12% to 18.4 on hawkish Fed re-statement
XLC -2.8% and XLY -2.5% leading sector losses; META -5.4%, AMZN -3.5%
SMH +1.3% and XBI +3.0% bucking the tape — AVGO +4.3% and biotech strength on UniQure Huntington's gene therapy news

discoveries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing peer rallying on UniQure FDA-pathway news plus its own FD disclosure
discoveries.thesis: NTLA up modestly today but a notable multi-day run (extended five-day gain) in sympathy with UniQure's announcement that it will seek FDA approval for its Huntington's gene therapy. Combined with NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01), this looks like a coherent, justified re-rating of gene-edit/gene-therapy regulatory optionality rather than an overreaction. Risk-off tape did not prevent the move — XBI +3% confirms group bid.
discoveries.what_confirms: NTLA's 8-K exhibit contains positive clinical update or partner milestone; continued XBI strength tomorrow; sell-side notes citing UniQure read-through to in vivo editing platforms.
discoveries.what_kills: NTLA 8-K turns out to be a routine conference presentation deck with no new data; UniQure FDA process stalls or pushback from agency reappears; XBI rolls over with broader risk-off resumption.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Regulatory thaw signals for one gene-therapy/edit name (especially after a public FDA fight) tend to lift the whole platform cohort; check 7.01/8.01 filings in adjacent names within 48h of the headline.
discoveries.catalyst: UniQure announcing FDA filing for Huntington's gene therapy lifting gene-editing group; NTLA Reg FD 8-K on 6/15
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K items 7.01+8.01+9.01 indicate a disclosed presentation/update coincident with sector-wide regulatory tailwind from UniQure headline.
research_pointers: Read the actual exhibit 99 in NTLA's 6/15 8-K to determine whether it is data, partner update, or conference deck
Check BEAM, CRSP, VERV for similar sympathy moves and disclosure patterns
Track UniQure (QURE) regulatory timeline as a leading indicator for in vivo gene therapy sentiment

discoveries.name: Saia, Inc.
discoveries.setup: LTL trucking name down hard with no SEC filing or scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: Sharp multi-day decline on no visible catalyst — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings. XLI is roughly flat (-0.14%), so this is not sector tape. Could be a monthly LTL tonnage update, competitor preannouncement, or analyst downgrade not captured in the news feed. Cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
discoveries.what_confirms: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first
discoveries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possible LTL tonnage data, downgrade, or peer preannouncement
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; XLI flat eliminates sector explanation, so move is company- or sub-industry-specific.
research_pointers: Check ODFL, XPO, ARCB for parallel moves — if peers down similarly, LTL data point is the driver
Search for analyst downgrades or June freight volume releases (ATA tonnage, Cass)
Check options flow for unusual put activity suggesting preannouncement risk

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Quality SaaS compounder down meaningfully over a week with no filings
discoveries.thesis: Multi-day decline in a software name with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. XLK is only down 0.34% so this is not pure tech tape. Could be sell-side derating on supply-chain software demand concerns, a competitor read-through, or a slow-bleed sentiment shift; without an identifiable catalyst I cannot commit to overdone/underdone at conf 3+.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; XLK relatively stable so move is name-specific.
research_pointers: Check for recent sell-side notes on supply-chain software (DSGX, SPSC, BL peers)
Look at insider Form 4 activity over the past two weeks
Review whether any large customer (retailer) issued a guide-down that implicates IT spend

discoveries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
discoveries.setup: Consumer cyclical name selling off in line with weak XLY tape
discoveries.thesis: DECK down on below-average volume on a day XLY is -2.5% and the broader risk-off impulse from a more hawkish Fed is hitting discretionary hardest. No company-specific catalyst, no 8-K, no earnings. Magnitude is roughly consistent with sector beta plus mid-cap discretionary skew — nothing to fade or chase.
discoveries.what_confirms: XLY continues to lead losses; peer footwear/apparel names (NKE, ONON, BIRK) show similar declines
discoveries.what_kills: DECK accelerates lower on rising volume tomorrow without sector follow-through, suggesting an undisclosed name-specific issue
discoveries.catalyst: Sector risk-off in consumer discretionary on hawkish Fed re-statement
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, below-average volume, and XLY -2.5% together indicate beta-driven move.
research_pointers: Compare DECK move to ONON and BIRK to confirm sector-driven explanation
Watch for any HOKA channel checks or UGG seasonal commentary in coming days

discoveries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Biotech rallying on company-specific 8.01 disclosure into a strong XBI tape
discoveries.thesis: PTCT up sharply on roughly 2x volume with an 8-K filed yesterday (items 8.01+9.01, i.e., 'other event' with exhibits — typically a press release for a regulatory update, partnership, or pipeline news). XBI +3% provides a supportive tape. Move appears appropriate to a non-trivial disclosure rather than excessive; without reading the exhibit I can't argue it's underdone, but the combination of own filing + group strength makes the magnitude defensible.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit reveals a meaningful regulatory or partnership milestone (e.g., FDA action date, EMA opinion, licensing deal)
discoveries.what_kills: Exhibit is routine corporate update with no new value-driving content — would suggest the move overshot and is faded once read
discoveries.catalyst: PTCT 8-K (8.01) filed 6/16 plus broader biotech bid
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8.01 + 9.01 filing pattern is the standard wrapper for a press release exhibit; coincident with XBI strength.
research_pointers: Read exhibit 99 attached to the 6/16 8-K to identify the specific disclosure
Check pipeline calendar for upcoming PTCT PDUFA dates or trial readouts
Confirm whether move is concentrated in PTCT or shared with rare-disease peers (SRPT, IONS)

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing peer rallying in sympathy with UniQure FDA news
discoveries.thesis: BEAM up alongside NTLA on a strong XBI tape with no company-specific 8-K. The UniQure Huntington's FDA pathway announcement is a regulatory sentiment positive for the entire base-editing/in-vivo editing cohort. Magnitude appears proportional to a group re-rating rather than excessive.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued XBI strength; sell-side notes explicitly citing read-through to base editors; further gene-therapy regulatory positives
discoveries.what_kills: XBI rolls over with no follow-through; UniQure FDA process hits a snag; tape risk-off resumes broadly
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with UniQure FDA filing announcement
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + coincident moves with NTLA + XBI +3% on day of UniQure news.
research_pointers: Track CRSP, VERV alongside BEAM/NTLA for cohort confirmation
Review Beam's own PRECISION-1 sickle cell program timeline for upcoming milestones

catalyst_chains.primary_event: UniQure (QURE) announces it will seek FDA approval for Huntington's disease gene therapy after prior public FDA dispute — positive regulatory read-through for gene editing / in vivo gene therapy cohort
Warsh Fed removes cutting bias; 2-yr yield jumps; rate-sensitive sectors rerate

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump claims Iran nuclear deal concluded and Strait of Hormuz will be 'immediately opened'; touts falling oil prices
trump_signals.note: Energy weak today (XLE -1.25%) consistent with the de-escalation narrative. If Hormuz fully reopens and Iranian export risk premium evaporates, crude could decline further — bearish for E&P and oilfield services, marginally positive for refiners and consumer transports. Caveat: prior Trump claims about Iran outcomes have wobbled; the post itself notes the MOU 'might not be the kind of document I should be signing,' which limits durability.

ai_announcements.headline: Pinterest launches experimental AI shopping app 'Ask Pinterest'
ai_announcements.summary: Pinterest's new conversational AI shopping interface for product recommendations.

ai_announcements.headline: Google launches Gemini-powered smart home speaker at $99.99
ai_announcements.summary: Google reinvents smart speaker with conversational Gemini AI replacing rigid Assistant commands.

ai_announcements.headline: Enterprises questioning AI ROI; Uber blew through AI budget, some companies cut Claude licenses, Meta killed internal leaderboard
ai_announcements.summary: NEA partner says enterprises are pulling back on tokenmaxxing after costs spiked. This is a meta-signal about AI spending sustainability, not a specific product announcement, but could pressure AI-exposed software names.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Manhattan Associates' supply chain/WMS software has deep ERP integration; agentic AI threats to enterprise software are real but MANH's core workflow software is sticky. The -9% five-day move suggests sympathy with broader application-software AI-displacement fears (sympathy w/ peer concerns about agentic AI eating SaaS).
research_pointers: Check if MANH had specific guidance/analyst downgrade today vs pure sympathy move
Look at peer app-software names (SPSC, DSGX) for sympathy confirmation
MANH trades at premium multiple - vulnerable to multiple compression on AI-disruption narratives even without fundamentals changing

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic blackout makes world leaders nervous about US AI dependency; Anthropic-Trump feud may actually boost sales per Ramp data
ai_announcements.summary: Recent Anthropic service blackout cited by Macron/Modi at G7 as evidence of US AI kill-switch risk. Separately Ramp data suggests enterprise adoption of Claude growing despite political friction. Flagging as Anthropic-related call for bias grading.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large stock moves today (PTCT, NTLA, BEAM healthcare; SAIA trucking; DECK footwear) have no plausible link to today's AI news. SAIA -6.8% is industrials/freight-cycle driven, not AI. DECK is consumer discretionary. Biotech moves are sector-specific. Only MANH -5.2% has a defensible (though weak) AI-narrative link via enterprise software AI-displacement fears amplified by the NEA 'ROI reckoning' commentary. Quiet day for clean AI-driven equity dislocations.
